entries.title: Replication Best Practices in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: When defining and running replication processes, follow these recommendations. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: A/B Test Object Import and Export in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use the abtest.xsd schema to import and export A/B tests. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Access Alerts for Your B2C Commerce Site
entries.summary: Find all tasks and notifications in the Alerts section of your Business Manager homepage. See all tasks and notifications for your site in the Alerts module. Alerts can be shown in the Business Manager homepage or header, or sent directly to Slack channels.

entries.title: Access Control for Log Center Alerts
entries.summary: Verify that your role allows alert creation and management.

entries.title: Access WebDAV Files for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use the folder browser in Business Manager to easily view and download WebDAV files. You can also use a third-party WebDAV client to view, download, upload, and delete WebDAV files.

entries.title: Create an Access Key for Logins for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: For organizations using unified authentication, B2C Commerce offers an alternative form of authentication when logging into to Business Manager via external applications: WebDAV File Access, UX Studio Agent, User Login, Open Commerce API (OCAPI), and Protected Storefront Access These access keys act as a substitute for your unified authentication password. Access keys expire after a year. If you enter an access key incorrectly six times, you can't use that access key or your password to log in to that authentication scope.

entries.title: Activate a B2C Commerce Account in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Before you can activate an account, it must first be created for you by an account administrator. When the administrator creates your account, Account Manager sends a message to your email address. This email message serves as a starting point for activating your account.

entries.title: Activate the Connection Between Account Manager and Salesforce in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: After you enable identity federation, link Account Manager with your Salesforce org.

entries.title: Add an API Client in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Account administrators use Account Manager to create API clients. To access Salesforce Commerce API (Shopper Commerce API [SCAPI]) or Open Commerce API [OCAPI]), use an API client. Append the API client ID to the URLs that you use to access these APIs. The On-Demand Sandbox API also requires an API client.

entries.title: Purge a B2C Commerce API Client
entries.summary: Account Manager administrators can purge API clients with the Account Manager purge feature.

entries.title: Audit Log Events
entries.summary: Account Manager B2C Commerce audit log events are available in Log Center and provide visibility into changes to users, API clients, and organizations.

entries.title: Automated Linking for Unlinked Accounts In Account Manager and B2C Commerce
entries.summary: If you don’t want to enable the just-in-time provisioning, you can still take advantage of automatic activation and linking of new user accounts by creating the user accounts in Account Manager.

entries.title: Change Account Information in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Change your account information with Account Manager. You can't change your email address, but you can change other account values, such as your business phone number. Also, depending on the multi-factor authentication (MFA) verification method settings of your organization, you can register methods for verifying your identity when logging in to B2C Commerce applications.

entries.title: Configure Account Manager for Identity Federation in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Identity federation enables users to access multiple applications or systems with a single set of credentials. When you combine identity federation with a Salesforce My Domain, users can link their Salesforce Identity or third-party Identity and Access Management (IAM) credentials with Account Manager.

entries.title: Connect Salesforce Authenticator (Version 3 or Later) to Your Account for Identity Verification in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: The Salesforce Authenticator mobile app is a fast and easy verification method for multi-factor authentication (MFA) logins. Register the app to connect it to your Account Manager account. To receive and approve push notifications sent to your mobile device, Salesforce Authenticator requires that you secure your mobile device with a security pin or face ID.

entries.title: Create a User Account in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: You must be an account administrator to create an account. Each account must belong to one or more organizations, and you can create accounts only for organizations of which you are a member.

entries.title: Delete an Account in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: You can delete an account only if you are an account administrator and the account is within one of your organizations. When you disable an account, the user can't log in to services using their credentials. When you delete an account, Account Manager still stores the user in the database for security-related reasons.

entries.title: Removal of Deprecated Roles in Account Manager
entries.summary: Avoid assigning deprecated roles to users in B2C Commerce. These roles no longer provide access to B2C services, and Salesforce plans to remove them starting in Account Manager 3.9 (June 2026). Removing a deprecated role from a user doesn’t result in loss of functionality or access.

entries.title: Edit an Organization in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: As an Account Administrator, you can edit an organization to specify a password policy,  allow your users to link their Account Manager account to an existing account in your organization in the Salesforce Platform, and specify the multi-factor authentication (MFA) verification methods that are available for users to verify their identity when logging in to B2C Commerce B2C applications. MFA is required for all Account Manager users to improve security and reduce the risk of unauthorized access and account compromise.

entries.title: Edit a User Account in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Account Administrators can edit the details, access permissions, and status of users accounts in their organization.

entries.title: Email Notifications
entries.summary: Email notifications are sent to users based on how the account is created and the identity federation setting for your organization in Account Manager. This topic applies to Account Manager and B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Enable and Disable an API Client ID in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Account administrators can use Account Manager to enable and disable API clients. The Open Commerce API requires to be accessed with API clients. The API client ID needs to be appended to the URLs used to interact with the Open Commerce API. An API client is also required to use the On-Demand Sandbox API.

entries.title: B2C Commerce Accounts FAQ for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: B2C Commerce uses a unified account management system, Account Manager. This topic answers common questions about setting up and accessing your account using Account Manager.

entries.title: Identity Federation with Salesforce Identity in B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Salesforce My Domain
entries.summary: Identity federation enables users to access multiple applications or systems with a single set of credentials. Account Manager uses identity federation to enhance security and the app login experience.

entries.title: FAQ and Identity Federation Troubleshooting
entries.headings: Where can I find login flow details for different SSO and Identity Federation scenarios? • Why don't I see Account Manager as a Connected App in my Core Organization? • What happens when a user is assigned to multiple organizations? • Do I have to create users still in Account Manager when I have turned on Salesforce Identity? • What happens when my user account in Salesforce has a different email address than my user account in Account Manager? • Can you unlink a user from Salesforce Identity? • What happens when you turn off Salesforce Identity again? • If an AM Org setting has Identity Federation Enforced and your customer identity provider (IDP) (for example, Ping) is down, how can you switch from enforced to disabled to log in to B2C Commerce? • What happens when the user email is changed in Salesforce Identity? • Can you change the "Salesforce My Domain Subdomain Name"? • Why can't I access WebDav, cartridge upload, pipeline debugger, script debugger or protected storefront after I linked my account with Salesforce Identity?
entries.summary: Find answers to frequently asked Identify Federation questions for B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Install Account Manager in Your Salesforce Org in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Configure the Salesforce org where you want to use identity federation.

entries.title: Add an Account to Your Organization in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: As an Account Administrator, you can add user accounts to your organization.

entries.title: Just-In-Time User Provisioning In Account Manager and B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Just-in-time user provisioning automates creating a user account in Account Manager eliminating the need for admins to add users manually. It also links the account with the Salesforce Identity account used for single sign-on (SSO).

entries.title: Link an Account Manager Account to Salesforce Identity (SSO) in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Depending on the settings of your organization, you can link Account Manager accounts to Salesforce accounts managed in Salesforce Identity. If your organization enables this capability, you can link your Account Manager account to a Salesforce account when you log in to Account Manager. To unlink your account, contact your account administrator.

entries.title: Account Manager Audit Log Events
entries.headings: Before You Begin • Access Account Manager Audit Log Events • Understand Key Fields • Filter Audit Events • Common Use Cases
entries.summary: Log Center publishes audit log events for Account Manager, including changes to users, API clients, and organizations. Use filters and keyword searches to track specific field changes or action events within a selected time frame.

entries.title: Log In with Identity Federation Allowed
entries.summary: If you already have an account, you receive an email describing your options to either link your account or log in using your Account Manager credentials. This topic applies to Account Manager and B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Log In with Identity Federation Enforced
entries.summary: Before logging in for the first time, you receive an email with a login link. This topic applies to Account Manager and B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Log into Account Manager in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Account Manager offers two login options, link for single sign-on (SSO) or log in with your username.

entries.title: Select Contact Users to Receive Account Manager Security Notification Emails
entries.summary: Select up to five contact users to receive security email alerts about changes in B2C Commerce Account Manager.

entries.title: Manage Access of Partner Accounts in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Account administrators can use Account Manager to manage access of user accounts invited to the administrator's organization (for example, a partner account). You can control which B2C Commerce applications the invited account can access.

entries.title: Account Manager for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Account Manager creates, maintains, and disables B2C Commerce accounts. It also grants (or denies) access to selected applications based on the account's credentials. Account Manager supports two different types of users: account administrators and non-administrative users. Account administrators can do everything non-administrative users can do. Account administrators can also create accounts, disable accounts, and so on. At least one account administrator is assigned to each organization. Before you can use Account Manager, you must log in.

entries.title: Purge an Account in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: You can purge an account only if you are an account administrator and the account is within one of your organizations. When you purge an account, Account Manager removes the user record from its database.

entries.title: Purge an API Client from Account Manager
entries.summary: B2C Commerce Account Manager administrators can purge API clients that are set to the disabled status for seven days or more.

entries.title: Register a Security Key for WebAuthn Identity Verification in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Register a Fast Identity Online (FIDO) U2F or WebAuthn (FIDO2) compatible security key as a verification method for multi-factor authentication (MFA) logins. To verify your identity, insert your security key into the appropriate port on your computer or mobile device to complete verification. You can register the same security key with multiple service providers and multiple Salesforce orgs and accounts. You can also register one key per account.

entries.title: Register Verification Methods for Multi-Factor Authentication in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is an extra layer of protection beyond a single password.

entries.title: Remove Verification Methods for Multi-Factor Authentication in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: If you want to replace a multi-factor authentication (MFA) verification method, you can remove registered methods from your Account Manager account.

entries.title: Reset a Forgotten Password in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: You can reset your password if you forgot it. However, this procedure works only if you successfully activated your account. Define a new password even if your existing password has expired. After a password has expired, you can attempt to log in six times to set a new password before your account is temporarily locked. After successfully logging in with an expired password, immediately reset your password.

entries.title: Change Your Account Password in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: You are required to change your password every 90 days. Define a new password even if your existing password expired. After a password has expired, you can attempt to log in six times to set a new password before your account is temporarily locked. After you successfully log in with an expired password, immediately reset your password.

entries.title: Reset a User Account in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Account administrators can use Account Manager to reset a user's account. This operation is useful if the user forgets their password or if you want to unlink an Account Manager account from a Salesforce account. When you reset a user's account, you put the account in the same state it was in when it was initially created. The user must reactivate the account.

entries.title: Roles Managed in Account Manager
entries.summary: Use this table as a guide to the B2C Commerce Account Manager roles.

entries.title: Bulk Migration or Creation of Account Manager Users
entries.summary: An open source tool, Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC)-CI CLI (https://github.com/SalesforceCommerceCloud/sfcc-ci) provides user management bulk actions in B2C Commerce Business Manager and Account Manager.

entries.title: B2C Commerce Configurations
entries.summary: Complete SSO configuration.

entries.title: Configure an Azure AD Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) Provider
entries.headings: When you integrate Salesforce with Azure AD, you can:
entries.summary: This guide serves as a general integration overview for B2C Commerce. For precise instructions, Salesforce advises consulting the most up-to-date integration documentation specific to your Identity Provider (IDP), such as Azure IDP.

entries.title: Configure Duo as an Authentication Provider
entries.summary: This guide serves as a general integration overview for B2C Commerce. For precise instructions, Salesforce advises consulting the most up-to-date integration documentation specific to your Identity Provider (IDP), such as Duo.

entries.title: Design Considerations
entries.summary: Keep these design considerations in mind when you integrate B2C Commerce Account Manager and other Salesforce applications with your identity provider.

entries.title: Set up and Integrate OKTA with Salesforce Identity
entries.headings: Configuration Help
entries.summary: This guide serves as a general integration overview for B2C Commerce. For precise instructions, Salesforce advises consulting the most up-to-date integration documentation specific to your Identity Provider (IDP), such as Okta.

entries.title: Partners and Single Sign-On to Salesforce
entries.summary: Your company security policies drive partner login through your IDP (Identity Provider). Some IDPs offer guest logins that don’t require a license. Alternatively, your partners can log into B2C Commerce apps with their existing Account Manager credentials.

entries.title: Provisioning Users in Account Manager
entries.summary: The current version of single sign-on (SSO) doesn’t yet support deletion of user accounts between B2C Commerce Account Manager and Salesforce Identity. An Account Manager admin creates a profile and assigns access for each user to applications like Business Manager or Control Center.

entries.title: Integrate B2C Commerce with Single Sign-On
entries.headings: License Requirements • Other Requirements • Implement This Solution
entries.summary: To make sure the connection is secure and reduce friction, integrate B2C Commerce Account Manager and other Salesforce applications with your identity provider.

entries.title: Use Access Keys for WebDAV File, UX Studio Agent, User Login, Open Commerce API (OCAPI), and Protected Storefront Access
entries.summary: For organizations using unified authentication, B2C Commerce offers an alternative form of authentication when logging into to Business Manager via external applications.

entries.title: Account Manager Workflow
entries.headings: Login Flow with Salesforce Identity
entries.summary: Learn how data flows through the configurations when you integrate B2C Commerce Account Manager and other Salesforce applications with your identity provider.

entries.title: Undelete an Account in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: When an account is deleted, the deleted user can no longer log into any systems requiring Account Manager credentials. If you delete an account by mistake or as a security precaution, you can undelete the account. Only account administrators can undelete an account.

entries.title: Unlock an Account in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: After six unsuccessful attempts to log in to an account, Account Manager temporarily locks the account for 30 minutes. An account administrator can explicitly unlock locked accounts within the administrator’s organizations.

entries.title: User Management with Identity Federation In Account Manager and B2C Commerce
entries.summary: You can configure the Identity Federation settings of your organization so that Account Manager user accounts are automatically created, provisioned, and linked to the Salesforce Identity of your Salesforce Organization (Salesforce Identity Management (SFIDM)).

entries.title: Verify Your Identity with a TOTP Authenticator App in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Register a third-party authenticator app, like Microsoft Authenticator or Google Authenticator, as a verification method for multi-factor authentication (MFA) logins. The app generates a verification code called a time-based one-time password (TOTP).

entries.title: Verify Your Identity with Multi-Factor Authentication in B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Salesforce Authenticator App • Security Key • One-Time Password Generator App
entries.summary: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is a simple, effective mechanism for enhancing login security and safeguarding your users’ accounts against security threats. MFA is part of the B2C Commerce login experience and can’t be turned off.

entries.title: Add an SSL Certificate to an eCDN Zone and Configure DNS Mapping
entries.summary: Add SSL certificates to your hostnames for Zones, and map DNS values from B2C Commerce to your DNS provider account. Add eCDN managed certificates and self-managed certificates.

entries.title: Add Managed SSL Certificates
entries.summary: Add eCDN managed SSL certificates to your hostnames for zones, and map your origin endpoints.

entries.title: Add Payment Methods to a Payment Zone for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: To configure how shoppers complete purchases, customize payment methods for each payment zone based on country or currency.

entries.title: SiteGenesis Configuration for Salesforce Payments
entries.summary: With some limitations, add Salesforce Payments to SiteGenesis.

entries.title: Add Self-Managed SSL Certificates
entries.summary: Add self-managed SSL certificates to your hostnames for Zones, and map your origin endpoints.

entries.title: Add Stacked Proxy to the Firewall Allowlist
entries.summary: Use the CDN Zones API custom rules to selectively skip the web application firewall (WAF), other custom rules, rate limits, or security levels for third-party IP addresses that you specify.

entries.title: Configure Wildcard Custom Hostnames
entries.summary: Use a single wildcard custom hostname (for example, `*.merchantsite.com`) to cover multiple subdomains and reduce onboarding and management overhead in eCDN via Business Manager.

entries.title: Add Favorites to Business Manager
entries.summary: To make your work faster and more efficient, save Business Manager modules as favorites. Add up to 20 site-specific or global modules to the Business Manager home page. Site-specific modules are only available for the selected site.

entries.title: Add Hostnames (Subdomains)
entries.summary: Subdomains of the same zone can be configured on the same Salesforce B2C Commerce instance, or on different instances of different realms of the same retailer.

entries.title: Administering Your Organization for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: As an administrator, you control users and permissions. You’re responsible for import and export, third-party integration, batch processing, and data replication. Use the Control Center to manage instances. You can also manage client certificates within your environment. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Administration Data Replication in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: These tables map Business Manager Administration modules to data replication tasks. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Administrator Role in B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Retrieving Passwords • Import and Export • Business Manager Module Permissions • Business Manager Locale Permissions • Business Manager Functional Permissions • WebDAV Permissions • Assign a Price Adjustment Limit • Find All Users with a Particular Permission
entries.summary: The administrator role is the top-level role for an organization. This role is automatically created and can't be deleted directly or indirectly by import. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.
keywords: Module permissions grant access to specific Business Manager modules. If a logged-in user has permission to access a Business Manager module, the application appears in the left navigation pane.
Business Manager supports localization of business objects. Specify which users have access to a locale's data.
Functional permissions aren't associated with a B2C Commerce tool module. They let a user perform specific functions in B2C Commerce.
To grant access to specific WebDAV folders, use WebDAV permissions. WebDAV permissions apply to your entire organization.
Assign limits per user for manual price adjustments in Business Manager. You can select a context of one, multiple, or all sites for which the limits apply.
Check which users have read or write access to a module.

entries.title: Limit Storefront Order Allowlist FAQ
entries.summary: Unauthorized access to storefront orders happens when an order is accessed through a storefront session using a different customer ID than the one it was created with. This makes it possible for unauthorized users to view the order’s details.

entries.title: Analyze HTTP and Security Traffic for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: To get insights into HTTP traffic and security data, use the eCDN Analytics tab in Business Manager. The Analytics tab shows the Traffic Analysis timeline chart, top traffic statistics across multiple dimensions, and per-rule activity. Switch between HTTP and Security views to see request traffic or firewall events for the same zone and time range.

entries.title: Anomaly Detection in Log Center
entries.summary: Identify performance regressions, operational issues, and suspicious activity from unusual patterns in Log Center. Log Center continuously analyzes incoming log data and compares the data with learned baseline behavior. When data deviates from the baseline and meets your thresholds, Log Center flags the anomaly. This topic applies to B2C Commerce. Use these guidelines to reduce false positives and improve detector accuracy.

entries.title: Apply Defense in Depth
entries.summary: Defense in depth means using multiple layers of security within your environment instead of just one. If an attack causes one security mechanism to fail, other mechanisms can still provide the necessary security to protect the system. Think of creating a security onion and not a security egg. When dropped, an egg smashes everywhere, while an onion just gets a little bruised because it has multiple layers.

entries.title: Archiving in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: You can't automatically roll back instances impacted by data import. Therefore, it’s a good practice to create an archive of your existing site that you can roll back to if the import fails. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Assign Granular Replication Permissions
entries.summary: Replicate specific products from staging to production.

entries.title: Assign Payment Zones to a Merchant Account for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Assign one or more payment zones to your merchant accounts. The default payment zone, set to all currencies and countries, is assigned to the first merchant account you create. Assign more payment zones to your merchant accounts to further customize which payment methods can be used for which zones.

entries.title: Assign an Inventory List to a Site
entries.summary: Assign an inventory list to one or more sites. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Associate an Adyen Merchant Account with B2C Commerce
entries.summary: To use Salesforce Payments with Adyen, associate your existing Adyen merchant account with your B2C Commerce instance. If you don’t have an Adyen merchant account, obtain one from Adyen first. You can’t create an Adyen merchant account through Salesforce Payments.

entries.title: Associate a PayPal Merchant Account with B2C Commerce
entries.summary: To use Salesforce Payments with PayPal, associate your PayPal merchant account with your B2C Commerce instance.

entries.title: Associate a Stripe Merchant Account with Your B2C Commerce Instance Commerce
entries.summary: To use Salesforce Payments with Stripe, create a Stripe merchant account directly from your B2C Commerce instance. You can also use a Stripe merchant account that’s already associated with a B2C Commerce instance.

entries.title: Authentication and Authorization in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Authentication and authorization are key security concepts. To configure security controls, you need access to Business Manager.

entries.title: Automating File Transfer Through Scripts in B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Creating File Transfer Pipelines in B2C Commerce • Creating File Transfer Scripts in B2C Commerce • Zipping and Unzipping Files Using B2C Commerce Script • Using WebDAVClient to Integrate Backend Systems with B2C Commerce • Using FTPClient to Integrate Backend Systems with B2C Commerce • Using HTTPClient to Integrate Backend Systems with B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use B2C Commerce scripts to pull files into the cartridge.
keywords: File transfer pipelines use B2C Commerce script to create a B2C Commerce FTP, HTTP, or WebDAV client and GET or POST files from an external system to the B2C Commerce instance. You can delete files from the external system after they’re successfully imported.
Use WebDAVClient, FTPClient, or HTTPClient in your file transfer script to move files from your backend system into B2C Commerce.
If you have large files, we recommend that you zip them for file transfer. To automate the zip process when using a pipeline to transfer files, create scripts. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.
B2C Commerce implements a WebDAV client in the B2C Commerce Script API to enable developers to access remote locations via the WebDAV protocol.

B2C Commerce implements an FTP client in the B2C Commerce Script API to enable developers to access remote locations via the FTP protocol. The FTPClient class can be used to pull or push files that don't require secure transmission. Because it isn't a secure transfer protocol, we recommend that you use the FTPClient only for sandboxes.
Use the HTTPClient class to create an HTTP client. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Automate Import/Export in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Before you start automation, decide on your file naming convention for import files. We recommend that you use a regular expression to name your import files and include a date signifier. This approach makes it easier to identify if the correct files were transferred to the instance and to archive files. Also decide how you want to archive your files and the naming convention for archived files. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Availability Calculation
entries.headings: Base Product and Variation Availability Calculation • Product Bundle Availability Calculation • Product Set Availability Calculation
entries.summary: A product's availability is calculated from its inventory and the stock update mechanism. If you use Salesforce Omnichannel Inventory, then Omnichannel Inventory calculates the availability of individual products, and the integration sends the data to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Available to Sell (ATS) Calculation
entries.headings: Standard Product ATS Calculation • Base Product and Variation ATS Calculation • Product Bundle ATS Calculation • Product Set ATS Calculation
entries.summary: In B2C Commerce, the product availability presented to storefront customers is defined as the available to sell (ATS) quantity.

entries.title: Best Practices for Import/Export in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: We recommend that you follow established best practices for import and export. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Business Manager Module Data Replication in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: The tables in this section map the data managed in Business Manager modules to data replication tasks. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Build Your Site Structure
entries.summary: When building your site, the order that you collect and generate the underlying product, content, and promotional qualifier information in matters. In general, create your catalog, product and pricing information first, and then create a marketing program to promote your products. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Business Manager Email Configurator
entries.summary: Use the optional email settings to define your own SMTP server for customer and platform email delivery. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Business Manager Import and Export Privileges in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Business Manager provides many levels of system privileges, and with that comes many separate importing and exporting modules. However, the functionality presented in the Business Manager is similar for each module. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Change the User Interface Locale for Business Manager
entries.summary: The Business Manager user interface supports multiple languages.

entries.title: Business Manager Network Access in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: To limit network access to Business Manager, configure security controls to restrict access to specific IP addresses or ranges. The intent of setting these network access restrictions is to lock out attackers who obtained valid credentials through illegitimate means. To enable this functionality, you can specify a range of allowlisted IP addresses allowed to access Business Manager and a range of blocklisted IP addresses not allowed to access Business Manager. If an IP address is blocklisted and allowlisted, it’s denied access.

entries.title: Browser Support
entries.summary: B2C Commerce supports several different browsers.

entries.title: Buy Now and Pay Later Payment Services for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: The buy now, pay later payment option for B2C Commerce makes it convenient for shoppers to purchase items in all price ranges. Salesforce Payments offers Buy Now, Pay Later payment through Klarna and AfterPay.

entries.title: Buy Now Pay Later with Afterpay for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Afterpay is available in Salesforce Payments through your Stripe Merchant account as a Checkout payment method. With Afterpay, shoppers make payment in interest-free installments. This feature is available for B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Buy Now Pay Later with Klarna for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Klarna is available as a Checkout payment method. With Klarna, shoppers can spread the cost of their order over three or four interest-free payments. To review the countries where Klara is available, see the Stripe documentation. This feature is available for B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Buy Now and Pay Now Checkout Methods for B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Buy Now • Pay Now
entries.summary: Salesforce Payments has two express checkout experiences: Pay Now and Buy Now. Pay Now requires a current shopper basket while Buy Now ignores the current basket.

entries.title: Cache Settings Object Import and Export in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use the cache settings object to manage static cache time to live, cache enablement, and page cache partitions. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: SiteGenesis Cartridges
entries.summary: A cartridge is a mechanism for packaging and deploying program code and data. You use cartridges to extend business functionality or integrate with external systems. A cartridge can deliver generic or application-specific functionality.

entries.title: Catalog Object Import/Export in B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Business Manager Import/Export Location • Catalog Import • Catalog Export • Page Meta Tags Import/Export • Pipelets • Schema Elements • Import/Export for Recommendations in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Import an entire catalog at once; the import operation includes the catalog and any categories and products within the catalog. Export works similarly; you export the entire catalog at once. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.
keywords: Recommendations are imported as part of the catalog that owns them. A recommendation is represented as a top-level element of the catalog schema. The recommendation element is a sibling of the <product>, <category> and <category-assignment> elements. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Private Keys and Certificates in B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Private Key Entry • Trusted Certificate Entry • Certificate Rotation
entries.summary: In various scenarios, you must import your third-party certificates and private keys into your instance’s keystore. A keystore can store multiple entries, each of which can be of type `PrivateKeyEntry` or of type `TrustedCertificateEntry`. This is similar to the terminology used by `java.security.KeyStore`.

entries.title: Change User Information in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Both users and administrators can edit user information in B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Clear eCDN Cache
entries.summary: B2C Commerce stores static storefront content in eCDN cache or the web tier static content cache.

entries.title: Clear Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) Known Good Hosts for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Business Manager remembers hosts previously used for SFTP. Clear these remembered known hosts.

entries.title: SiteGenesis Client-Side Scripts
entries.summary: Client-side js performs functions that don’t require information from the server.

entries.title: Code Replication in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Code replication transfers a code version from staging to a development or production instance and activates it on the target instance. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Code Replication Processes in B2C Commerce

entries.title: Code Upload in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Upload custom code, also known as cartridges, to an instance using UX Studio, or with a file transfer mechanism such as WebDAV over Transport Layer Security (TLS). It’s important that you secure code uploads. Follow these best practices.

entries.title: Commerce Script Injection in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Server script injection, or remote code execution, is a class of attack where maliciously crafted input is run in a privileged code execution context. This class of attack enables an attacker to control the code executed on the server.

entries.title: B2C Commerce Security Guide
entries.summary: To help protect your sites from security threats, follow our security best practices as you set up, administer, and develop your Salesforce B2C Commerce environments.

entries.title: B2C Commerce Security Model
entries.summary: Salesforce B2C Commerce security is a partnership between Salesforce and our B2C Commerce customers. As always, Trust is our #1 value at Salesforce. We’re committed to delivering the highest standard in system availability, performance, and security.

entries.title: Configuration for Administrators
entries.summary: Log Center administrators can access the Config tab to set log levels and log volumes.

entries.title: Configure B2C Commerce Alerts in Business Manager and Slack
entries.summary: Set up notifications for your organization in Business Manager and Slack. Configure updates, such as security vulnerabilities, campaign and promotion updates, or site alerts, to appear on your Business Manager homepage. You can also send updates directly to your Slack workspaces.

entries.title: Configure and Run a Log Center Search
entries.summary: Search for messages from system and custom code.

entries.title: Configure Basket Preferences

entries.summary: Configurable basket settings include persistence across shopper sessions, the treatment of duplicate product line items, and the maximum number of product line items per basket. These preferences are site-specific, and a customized storefront doesn’t necessarily evaluate them.

entries.title: Configure Business Manager Email Settings
entries.summary: To define your own SMTP server for customer and platform email delivery, configure email settings. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Create cross-site request forgery (CSRF) Allowlists for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Configure how your site handles CSRF allowlists.

entries.title: Configure a Page Shield Policy for the eCDN
entries.summary: Set up a Page Shield policy to make sure only trusted scripts run on sensitive store pages like cart and checkout. A Page Shield policy helps your store comply with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) 4.0 client-side security requirements.

entries.title: Configure the Enforce HTTPS Global Preference
entries.summary: Enforce the use of HTTPS for all sites in an instance. When this setting is enabled, URLs generate using the HTTPS protocol, and incoming page requests that use HTTP redirect to HTTPS. HTTP requests to Open Commerce API (OCAPI)'s session bridge aren't accepted. Also, instead of a combination of session cookies and secure tokens, secure session cookies are used, which helps avoid incorrect (false positive) session hijacking detections. Enable the Enforce HTTPS global preference to let browsers send cookies in cross-site contexts. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Set Up Eventing in B2C Commerce (Pilot)
entries.summary: Use eventing in B2C Commerce to receive notifications when certain data or processing events occur in your Commerce Cloud environment. You enable event types and configure connectors in Business Manager; B2C Commerce routes events directly to your configured connectors.

entries.title: Configure HSTS for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) secures your site by instructing web browsers to access your domain using only HTTPS. HSTS prevents attackers from using downgrade attacks against your site. For extra security, enable preload, which forces web browsers to open your site in HTTPS the first time it's requested. Read the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) on HSTS for more information.

entries.title: Set HSTS for Business Manager in Global Preferences
entries.summary: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) can substantially improve the security of the Business Manager. To secure Business Manager, HSTS instructs web browsers to access the domain using only HTTPS.

entries.title: Migrate Users to Unified Authentication via Account Manager
entries.summary: To let your users log in to their instances via Account Manager, migrate them to Unified Authentication. After you migrate them, users manage only one set of login credentials. To increase security on your instances, use Account Manager's two-factor authentication. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Configure Payment Zones for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Create payment zones to assign payment methods to specific currencies and countries for B2C Commerce. After you create a payment zone, assign it to a merchant account. Then, select the payment methods for the zone.

entries.title: Configure Salesforce Payments with Adyen for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Integrate your existing Adyen merchant accounts into Salesforce Payments in any B2C Commerce sandbox, staging, development, or production instance. Connect any B2C Commerce instance to a test or live Adyen merchant account.

entries.title: Configure Salesforce Payments with PayPal for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Configure the PayPal Payment Service Provider (PSP) with Salesforce Payments for B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Configure Salesforce Payments with Stripe for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Configure Stripe merchant accounts with Salesforce Payments in any B2C Commerce sandbox, staging, development, or production instance. Stripe merchant accounts that you create in a B2C Commerce instance can operate in both test and live mode.

entries.title: Configure Shopper Profile Sync
entries.summary: Use Shopper Profile Sync to synchronize registered shoppers between B2C Commerce and your Salesforce org as person accounts.

entries.title: Add a System Use Notification Message in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Create a system use notification message that displays when your users log in. You can also require them to acknowledge this message before continuing to log in.

entries.title: Configure the Embedded CDN
entries.headings: eCDN on On-Demand Sandboxes (ODS)
entries.summary: The embedded CDN (eCDN) is a geographically distributed network of proxy servers. Configuring the eCDN improves availability and performance, ultimately enhancing the ability to scale your site (or sites).

entries.title: Analytics for an eCDN Zone
entries.headings: What's New in Configure Zones Analytics • Accessing Zone Analytics
entries.summary: View HTTP and security traffic metrics for an eCDN zone, including the Traffic Analysis timeline chart and top statistics, using the Configure Zones interface in Business Manager.

entries.title: Configure Crypto Settings for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: Configure SSL/TLS and cryptography settings for an eCDN zone, including Transport Layer Security (TLS) version and HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS), using the Configure Zones interface in Business Manager.

entries.title: Configure Custom Error Pages for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: Set custom HTML error pages for 500-class and 1000-class errors generated by the embedded CDN for a zone.

entries.title: Configure Firewall Settings for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: Configure firewall settings for an eCDN zone using the Configure Zones interface in Business Manager.

entries.title: Configure Host Header Override Rules for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: Create rules that override the HTTP Host header sent to your origin server for an eCDN zone, using the Configure Zones interface in Business Manager.

entries.title: Configure Logpush and Logpull for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: Stream eCDN request, security, and Page Shield logs to your own storage or observability platform (Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, Azure Blob Storage, Datadog, or Splunk), or download recent logs on demand - all from the Logs tab on the Configure Zone screen in Business Manager.

entries.title: Configure Notifications for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: Get notified when eCDN events that matter to you happen, such as a Logpush job failure on a PCI-bound zone, a security incident, or a configuration change, without having to watch dashboards. The Notifications tab on the Configure Zone screen lets you configure webhook destinations (such as a Slack incoming webhook or your own HTTPS endpoint) and wire them up to one or more zones.

entries.title: Configure Page Shield Policies for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: Configure Page Shield policies that detect and identify supply chain attacks affecting your storefront, using the Configure Zones interface in Business Manager.

entries.title: Realm Security Rules for eCDN Zones

entries.summary: Realm Security Rules let you define Managed IP Address Lists and Custom Firewall Rules that control how trusted IP traffic is handled across your eCDN zones. These rules replace the legacy Trusted IP configuration.

entries.title: Configure Speed Settings for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: Optimize zone performance by enabling speed settings such as Early Hints, HTTP/3, and image compression via the Configure Zones interface in Business Manager.

entries.title: Configure WAF Settings for an eCDN Zone
entries.summary: Configure Web Application Firewall (WAF) managed rulesets for an eCDN zone by using the Security Rules tab in Configure Zones in Business Manager.

entries.title: Configure an eCDN Zone in Business Manager
entries.summary: Configure SSL/TLS, certificates, security rules, performance, custom pages, request logs, and Under Attack Mode for an eCDN zone by using the Configure Zone screen in Business Manager.

Deploy your CDN (or a reverse proxy) in front of B2C Commerce to improve performance and security, or to provide extra functionality using your CDN.

entries.title: Configure Commerce Intelligence Preferences for IP Filtering
entries.summary: Internal performance and load testing tools can contaminate the data you see in Reports and Dashboards, making it difficult to distinguish between generated traffic and actual customer behavior. Specify IP address ranges to filter out traffic from your internal testing tools, ensuring that your analytics reflect genuine customer interactions.

entries.title: Configure the eCDN Hostname Alias

entries.title: Configure Organization Locales for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Salesforce B2C Commerce is structured as an organization containing one or more sites (storefronts). Define localization settings for both entities.

entries.title: Configure Page Meta Tags in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Create page meta tags, which can be used within catalogs and libraries to create rule base page meta tag content.

entries.title: Configure Product and Content Locking for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Define the time (in minutes) for when products or content are locked while editing. During this time, other users can't edit the product record or content asset. The lock (applied before product editing) is released after this time.

entries.title: Configure Retention Settings for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Configure the lifetime of inventory and product price records that no longer reference products. You can also configure the lifetime for lists created by anonymous shoppers to retain this information for a longer time period. This information is typically for wish lists and gift registries.

entries.title: Configure Site Locales for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Salesforce B2C Commerce is structured as an organization containing one or more sites (storefronts). Define locale settings in Business Manager for these two entities, first at the organization level and then at the site level.

entries.title: Configuring Storefront Sites for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: New sites require some level of access control. Many use a single shared password for all users. Using a Business Manager login with the functional permission provides a much higher level of security and access control, in particular with frequent password renewals and employees leaving an organization.

entries.title: Configure Storefront URL Preferences
entries.summary: Enable your SEO URL rules for a site.

entries.summary: Send alerts and notifications directly to Slack channels, when you connect your workspaces to Business Manager.

entries.summary: Create a trusted data exchange between B2C Commerce and a Salesforce org. By connecting your B2C Commerce instance to Salesforce, you can access agentic and cross-cloud experiences, such as Shopper Agent, Merchant Agent, Shopper Profile Sync, and Shopper Notifications.

Controllers manage the flow of data in your application, and create ViewModels to process each storefront request as a route and generate an appropriate response. For example, in a storefront application, clicking a category menu item or entering a search term triggers a controller that renders a page.

entries.title: Support Cookie SameSite Attribute Changes for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Many browser vendors, for example Google Chrome, have introduced a new default cookie attribute setting of SameSite=Lax. Previously, the SameSite cookie attribute defaulted to SameSite=None. When SameSite is set to None, cookies require the Secure attribute indicating that they require an encrypted HTTPS connection. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

When you route Log Center notifications in B2C Commerce to PagerDuty, the alert's state lifecycle is mapped to a specific PagerDuty action and severity. Each alert monitor uses a unique deduplication key, so repeated triggers update the existing open incident instead of creating duplicate incidents.

entries.summary: When a custom SSL certificate isn’t installed to your development, staging, or production instance POD, or a custom SSL certificate installed to the POD doesn't cover the hostname, create an eCDN zone, and upload the certificate for each domain name.

entries.title: Create a Code Replication Process in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Trigger a code replication process manually, schedule it to run at a specified time, or create a job to run it.

entries.summary: Configure a custom firewall rule to control incoming traffic based on specified request parameters.

entries.summary: Create a parameter to use in different job steps. For example, create a parameter for ReplicationProcessID and set it to the ID of a specific replication process. Then use the parameter in different job steps. If you need to use a different replication process, change the value of the parameter, and B2C Commerce updates all steps that include the parameter. Overwrite the value of a job parameter if you use Open Commerce API (OCAPI) to execute a job. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Create a Job in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: When you create a job, you configure at least one flow, and select which steps to execute in each flow. Use out-of-the-box system job steps that don't require any coding. If there isn't a system job step available to do what you want, have a developer create a custom job step. You can also schedule when the job executes, specify resources to lock during job execution, and configure email notifications and error handling.

entries.summary: Legacy jobs use pipelines that a developer creates using the UX Studio plug-in to the Eclipse IDE. This functionality is deprecated. We recommend creating jobs using the new job framework.

entries.title: Create a Zone in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: The CDN configuration is organized around zones. A zone represents a root or apex domain (for example, example.com). A hostname is a subdomain of a specific zone (for example, www.example.com).

entries.title: Create Custom Preferences for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: In Salesforce B2C Commerce, you can create custom preferences for sites, and then view and edit them both locally and across multiple sites. Custom preferences enable site developers to make properties of the system modules configurable in Business Manager.

entries.title: Creating Data Files for Import in B2C Commerce

entries.summary: When the data structure and scope is understood and documented, map how to translate existing data into the B2C Commerce format. Also, map how to export the data from the B2C Commerce environment back into the customer's environment.

entries.title: Create Global Custom Preferences for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: In Salesforce B2C Commerce, you can create global custom preferences, and then view and edit them. Custom preferences enable organization developers to make properties of the system modules configurable in Business Manager.

entries.title: Creating Import/Export Pipelines in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: In addition to importing a file, you can also use your import/export pipeline to update search indexes, archive files, and copy bad import files to a specific directory. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Create Inventory Lists and Records
entries.summary: Use Business Manager to create inventory lists and records. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Creating Jobs in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use Business Manager to create a job. We recommend that you use the new job framework to create jobs, but the legacy, deprecated functionality is also available in Business Manager.

entries.summary: A role is a named group of permissions. For example, group all of the permissions related to catalog management into a role called Catalog Manager. Next, assign this role to the user whose job it is to manage the catalog. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Credit Card Encryption in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Credit card encryption, key generation, and re-encryption are automatic in B2C Commerce. B2C Commerce provides the ability to monitor this process for merchants undergoing a PCI audit.

entries.title: Cross Cloud Trust for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Tenant groups enable easy configuration and trust setup across clouds and services. A Salesforce super tenant can add a B2C Commerce realm as a member of its tenant group. Members of a tenant group can share data among themselves without requiring configuration of various one-way trust relationships. This simplifies creation of data sharing policies. An anchor tenant creates a tenant group. For B2C Commerce, the anchor tenant is always a Salesforce tenant and is called as a super tenant. The super tenant owns the tenant group and its memberships. Creation of supertenant and tenant groups is done in Salesforce. Salesforce is also responsible for adding or removing members from the tenant group. Salesforce initiates a two-way handshake with B2C Commerce to add it to a tenant group. This two-way handshake involves the B2C Commerce cross-cloud tenant group provisioning service. Each B2C Commerce tenant can be a member of only one tenant group.

entries.title: Cross-Site Request Forgery in B2C Commerce

Authenticated shoppers who change their accounts or submit personal data to a server to complete an action typically perform these requests. Users expect that they alone make these requests, and only when they specifically initiate the request.

entries.title: Cross-Site Scripting in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Cross-site scripting (XSS) enables attackers to inject client-side JavaScripts into a webpage viewed by a targeted user. To prevent malicious attacks through content manipulation, you must properly encode all user-provided content.

entries.summary: Any URL that is already configured in service credentials is automatically added to your allowlist. Follow this process to configure new endpoints and add them to your allowlist.

entries.title: Data Upload in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Data upload refers to transferring data files from an external system to your instance’s file system. Upload data such as import files, images, and snapshots manually or programmatically. Use Business Manager to transfer data manually. As always, any interaction with Business Manager occurs over HTTPS. Custom code performs data transfer programmatically. In this case, while several file transfer protocols are supported, use only the secure protocols: WebDAV over Transport Layer Security (TLS) (recommended), HTTPS, or Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP).

entries.title: Denial-of-Service Protection in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: In a denial-of-service (DoS) attack, the attacker attempts to deny computer resources to a network’s users. Attackers usually accomplish this by overwhelming the target computer system’s resources with unauthorized requests, which prevent valid users from accessing the network.

entries.title: Determine How to Block Attacks in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: While Salesforce B2C Commerce provides security protections, customers are responsible for configuring security controls and for not removing security controls that are enabled by default. entries.summary: Process-level replication events fire when a replication process reaches a terminal state. Use them to track when bulk replication jobs complete or fail.

entries.title: Replication Error States

entries.summary: Failed process-level replication events (`sf.cc.replication.failed`) include an `errorState` field with a machine-readable code that identifies the failure. Use this page to look up what an error state means.

entries.summary: The system fires granular replication events when the granular replication job completes, partially fails, or fully fails. Granular replication operates at the per-object level (products, price tables, content assets) rather than the bulk replication-process level, so its events report which individual object IDs succeeded and which failed.

entries.summary: Replication events carry one or more replication groups in their payload. Each replication group identifies a unit of data that participated in the replication. Use this page to look up what a given group ID covers.

entries.summary: The system fires a shopper registered event when a new customer profile is successfully created and committed. This event contains core customer information needed by external systems. Unlike replication events, which operate at the organization level, the shopper registered event is site-specific, so you can register and route it independently per site.

This service provides support for DKIM and SPF authentication. DKIM is an email authentication protocol that adds a cryptographic digital signature to outgoing emails. With DKIM, receivers can verify that the domain owner authorized the email and that it wasn’t altered in transit. DKIM helps reduce spam and phishing emails.

entries.title: Locking System Resources during Job Execution
entries.summary: When you create a job, prevent another job from modifying system resources while your job is executing. To know which system resources to assign to the job, know which resources are related to which entity. For example, if your job modifies customers, assign the system resource profile to your job because customers are related to the profile system resource. When your job is executing, another job can't modify the profile system resource and unexpectedly change the outcome of your job. Resource locks are re-entrant locks. In other words, if a job locks a resource, job steps in that job can lock the same resource again. The product index Rebuild task doesn't require any resource locks except itself. However, the product index update task requires resources locks on Product, Catalog and Category. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.summary: When you migrate to unified authentication, your users can log in to their Business Manager instances through Account Manager. Unified authentication streamlines the login process. Users receive a single set of login credentials to access all Business Manager instances. As a result, your users have fewer credentials to remember. This applies for administrators who are manually migrating users to unified authentication. This doesn’t apply to administers who started using B2C Commerce after October of 2019 because their organizations automatically use unified authentication.

entries.summary: Two-factor authentication (2FA) is an extra layer of protection beyond a single password. The extra protection can be knowledge (something you know), possession (something you have), or biometrics (something you are). Using a second authentication factor, such as a mobile phone (something you have), provides additional protection against common security threats such as account takeover, fraud, data loss, malicious code, and phishing attacks.

entries.title: Use a Positive Security Model
entries.summary: A positive security model, also known as a safelist approach, defines what is allowed and rejects everything else. Use this approach to make sure that you can allowlist only the known good input instead of trying to disallow all possible bad input.

entries.title: Use Secure Defaults
entries.summary: As an administrator, you want to make the user experience secure and reduce the number of default security settings. Configure security settings so that users must opt out of default security settings rather than opt in. If there's a security risk, clearly label insecure functionality in APIs or the user interface to discourage use.

Traditionally, we always used local user authentication, but a local user can perform only a local login into the Business Manager instance on which their credentials were created. After a site migrates to unified authentication, the site requires unified authentication on Account Manager, where a user can perform a global login into any Business Manager instance in their organization.

entries.summary: Salesforce B2C Commerce uses role-based access control to perform authorization. A user is assigned one or more roles, with each role consisting of a set of permissions. entries.title: Capturing Offline Orders and Inventory Reconciliation
entries.headings: Automatic Reconciliation • Reconciliation Reports
entries.summary: During Omnichannel Inventory service interruptions, Continuous Order Capture queues B2C Commerce orders to maintain business continuity. The system accepts orders based on the last known inventory data. To avoid overselling, B2C Commerce automatically decrements the Available-To-Order (ATO) value immediately after each order is created. The system marks these orders as Reservation Missing until Omnichannel Inventory recovers. After the connection is restored, the system automatically reconciles the inventory and creates reconciliation reports.

entries.title: Continuous Order Capture for Omnichannel Inventory with Salesforce Order Management
entries.summary: B2C Commerce Continuous Order Capture holds orders in a Reservation Missing state to prevent incomplete data from reaching Salesforce Order Management. After the Omnichannel Inventory service recovers, the system automatically reconciles the inventory and clears this Reservation Missing state. All orders are then pushed to Salesforce Order Management, even if they’re flagged as oversold due to partial or failed reservations.

entries.title: B2C Commerce Continuous Order Capture for Omnichannel Inventory
entries.summary: Turn on Continuous Order Capture to accept customer orders and maintain business continuity even if connectivity to Omnichannel Inventory is lost. When the service is unavailable, Continuous Order Capture automatically transitions to an offline mode and accepts transactions without immediate inventory reservations. By independently reconciling pending reservations and providing real-time recovery alerts, this feature protects your storefront from downtime while minimizing overselling risks.

entries.title: Enable Continuous Order Capture for B2C Commerce

entries.headings: Test Group Effects on the Storefront Experience
entries.summary: Distribute test segments across a maximum of 12 simultaneous A/B tests, as long as the total segments for all active A/B tests don’t exceed 12. Each A/B test can have up to five unique test segments, one of which is always the *control group.* Each test segment targets a portion of the total test participants. The control group receives the same storefront experience as if the A/B test did not exist. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: A/B Testing for B2C Commerce
entries.headings: A/B Testing vs. Personalization • A/B Testing Eligibility • Set Up Incremental Indexing • A/B Testing vs. Personalization • A/B Testing Eligibility • Eligibility and A/B Test Sample Size • A/B Test Data Deletion
entries.summary: Using Business Manager, you can assign promotions or slot content to an A/B test with common qualifiers such as source codes or customer groups. A/B test statistics use a combination of two-sided t-tests and chi-square tests to weigh test segments against each other and compute the statistical significance.
keywords: Set up incremental indexing for B2C Commerce.

Although the effect is similar, A/B testing segmentation is different from personalization segmentation. The regular personalization of a site, for example, done using customer groups, is segmented by criteria that you can define. Meanwhile, A/B test segmentation runs randomly. With both, the user sees an experience that is potentially different than other users. A/B testing, however, goes beyond personalization. A/B testing collects metrics to help determine which experience, among two or more, performs the best according to one or more segmentation criteria.

The Tracking site preference determines a shopper’s eligibility for A/B tests. The Tracking site preference is session-specific and only shoppers for whom tracking is allowed are eligible for A/B tests.

The consent requirement for A/B test eligibility and the possibility of a varied A/B test experience for unregistered shoppers can impact your test sample size. Consider planning longer test durations or altering your test criteria to capture statistically significant A/B test date.

Shopper data deletion requests cascade to A/B test participant records. When a shopper requests to have their records deleted from the system, all the shopper's A/B test participant records are deleted.

Use the Reports & Dashboards portal, to review your site's sales, product, promotion, technical, traffic, and other data. Each dashboard presents analytics that help you identify trends over time.

entries.summary: To record Analytics events in SFRA, you construct the corresponding URL patterns by calling helper methods in the `reportingUrls.js` module. Each of these helper methods constructs URLs and returns an array of URLs. This array is then passed as a variable (`pdict.reportingURLs`) to the template `reportingUrls.isml`.

The Salesforce Reference Architecture (SFRA) and SGJC reference applications use different techniques to construct the URLs.

entries.title: Assign Coupons to B2C Commerce Campaigns
entries.summary: To track and organize your promotions, assign coupons to one or more B2C Commerce campaigns. You can report on and organize coupons, and tie the coupon’s effective window to a campaign and promotion schedule. Instead of repeating the setup on each promotion, apply a coupon to all promotions under a campaign. You can still configure exceptions per promotion.

entries.title: Assign a Price Book or Promotion to a Specific Store in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: With Store-Specific Pricing and Promotions, select a price book for each of your stores, up to 2,000. The assignments framework lets merchants and developers set qualifiers, such as a store that activates certain experiences, such as a promotion. You can also assign a promotion to a specific store. This feature is only supported via XML import. To use a site assignment, a developer must first edit the assignments XML to include a store assignment. Currently only stores can be listed as assignments.

entries.title: Availability for Active Data
entries.headings: Standard Product • Base Product and Variations • Product Bundle • Product Set
entries.summary: Availability is the normalized ratio between the Allocation attribute and the Available To Sell (ATS) attribute of a product inventory record. Special rules apply for variation products, product sets, and potentially product bundles. Availability is the same for all sites that share an Inventory list, so it's basically a site-specific value. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.summary: Duplicate content is content that appears on a site for two (or more) URLs without a redirect. Even though the way search engines determine the search rating of a page changes frequently, most search engines decrease the search rating for such content. To have the best search ratings for your page, avoid duplicate content. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Campaigns and Promotions in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: In the Business Manager Online Marketing module, create campaigns and A/B tests that act as wrappers, containing experiences that include promotions, content slots, and sorting rules. Slot configurations and sorting rules, however, don't require assignment to campaigns or A/B tests to take effect. Slot configurations can be scheduled outside the context of an A/B test. A default sorting rule can be set for a category in a storefront catalog outside the context of a campaign or A/B test. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Cancel Orders
entries.summary: You can cancel an order that is in New, Open, or Completed Status. You can't cancel a failed order. To cancel an order associated with active post-processing objects, such as shipping orders, returns, or appeasements, you must cancel them. If you use B2C Commerce Order Management, then after an order is sent to Order Management, manage it there. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Catalog Design
entries.summary: In B2C Commerce Business Manager, you can enter product data manually or you can import the data in an XML file and edit the data. Most customers import product data from their Product Information Management system or other system of record, and then enhance it in Business Manager.

entries.summary: This topic applies to B2C Commerce. Here are some common ways you can configure and use catalogs to merchandise your products.

entries.title: Catalogs
entries.summary: This topic applies to B2C Commerce. B2C Commerce stores your product data in a catalog. A catalog is a collection of the products that you sell, organized into different categories.

entries.title: Categories for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: You create and organize categories and subcategories to organize and group products in your catalog and on your storefront. For example, an outdoors outfitter site uses Boots, Climbing Gear, and Outerwear categories to organize products on their storefront. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Categories and Products for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Assign products to more than one category within and across catalogs. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Categories and Storefront Navigation for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: With the proper settings, categories also provide your storefront navigation structure. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Category Attributes for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Modify the default category attributes, and create custom attributes that are appropriate for a category. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Category Linking for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Link categories to each other so items in one category can be identified as belonging to another category. For example, link an Accessories category to a Women's category. Use the link to identify certain products within the Women's category as Accessories, such as scarves, jewelry, belts, or shoes. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Category and Folder Slots Contained in Locally Included Templates in B2C Commerce

entries.title: Add a Content Block to a Page
entries.summary: Place a content block on a Page Designer page to reuse content across multiple pages.

entries.title: Apply Bulk Actions to Content Blocks
entries.summary: Select multiple content blocks and apply targeting or scheduling rules to all of them at once. Bulk actions reduce the time required to manage seasonal campaigns and sitewide content updates.

entries.title: Delete a Content Block
entries.summary: Permanently delete a content block from the Content Blocks module. Before deleting, review the content block's usage to confirm it isn't referenced by any active storefront pages or regions.

entries.title: Edit a Content Block
entries.summary: Edit a content block's components, attributes, or styling in Page Designer. Changes apply everywhere the content block is used across your storefront.

entries.title: Filter and Search Content Blocks
entries.summary: Use the Content Blocks module to browse, search, and filter content blocks. Filters let you narrow results by campaign, promotion, schedule, targeting locale, customer group, and creation date.

entries.title: Localize a Content Block
entries.summary: Create locale-specific versions of a content block so that localized content appears on your storefront for each target locale.

entries.title: Manage Content Blocks
entries.summary: Use the Content Blocks module in Business Manager to search, filter, target, and manage the lifecycle of content blocks from a centralized location.

entries.title: Content Blocks Overview
entries.headings: Benefits • How Content Blocks Work • Where Content Blocks Can Be Used • Content Blocks and Content Slots • Limitations
entries.summary: Content blocks are compositions of one or more components that you define one time and reuse across multiple pages and storefront regions. They use a JSON-based format and are supported for PWA Kit, Storefront Next, Salesforce Reference Architecture (SFRA), and SiteGenesis storefronts.

entries.title: Content Blocks in Page Designer
entries.summary: In Page Designer, save components as content blocks, add content blocks to pages, and remove content blocks from pages. Content blocks that you create or modify in Page Designer also appear in the Content Blocks module.

entries.title: Remove a Content Block from a Page
entries.summary: Remove a content block from a Page Designer page without affecting other pages where the content block is used.

entries.title: Create a Content Block
entries.summary: Create a content block by saving a component in Page Designer. The content block retains the component's content, attribute values, and targeting rules.

entries.title: Set Targeting Rules for a Content Block
entries.summary: Control when and to whom a content block is visible by adding it to a campaign or promotion, or by applying custom targeting rules.

entries.title: View Content Block Usage and References
entries.summary: See all the pages and storefront regions where a content block is currently used. Reviewing usage helps you understand the impact of changes before editing or deleting a content block.

entries.title: Change the B2C Commerce Dashboard Realm Currency
entries.summary: With B2C Commerce Reports and Dashboards, you set the realm currency setting so that aggregated realm data for all your sites shows in the selected currency.

entries.title: Change the B2C Commerce Dashboard Timezone
entries.summary: The default realm timezone setting for Reports & Dashboards is Greenwich Mean Time (GMT). You can change the timezone setting to one that better serves your business needs.

entries.title: Change History
entries.headings: Change History Details
entries.summary: Configure which B2C Commerce objects you want to track, and view the change history on staging and production instances. For each object, see when it was created, updated, or deleted.

entries.title: Characters Allowed in URLs

entries.summary: Offer your shoppers a choice of bonus product as a discount. For example, spend at least $1,000 on living room furniture and get your choice of three different types of televisions. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Choice-of-Bonus-Product Discount FAQ in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: The APIs described in the topic, Implementing a Choice-of-Bonus-Product Discount, are all that you need to implement this discount in your storefront. Use the following to address some common questions. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Choose Which Attributes Appear in the Category Grid View
entries.summary: By default, products in the grid view show the product's ID, name, status, and the product attributes catalog, and type. You can modify which product attributes appear on the product tiles. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Classification vs. Primary Category

entries.summary: When assigning a product to a category, the primary and classification categories are used to determine what information appears for the product in the storefront. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: B2C Commerce Data in Tableau
entries.headings: Salesforce B2C Commerce Cloud Connector • Common Data Terms • B2C Commerce Data Aggregations
entries.summary: Use your B2C Commerce data in Tableau to create dashboards and data views tailored to your specific business needs. The Salesforce B2C Commerce Cloud Connector exports your B2C Commerce analytics data to your Tableau instance. You can use Tableau's data visualization features to analyze your data without code.

entries.title: B2C Commerce Data Subject Areas
entries.summary: The Tableau connector provides access to data from the B2C Commerce subject areas. You can select a data subject area data visualization in Tableau. The subject areas are the same analytics data used to generate B2C Commerce Reports & Dashboards.

entries.title: Commerce Tableau Connector
entries.summary: This topic applies to B2C Commerce. The Commerce Tableau Connector facilitates access to your B2C Commerce base metrics in Tableau. Use Tableau to generate customized analytic views of your data.

entries.title: Common Phrases
