entries.summary: A common phrase is a search term with 2-5 words that are next to each other and in a specific order. Use common phrases to decrease noise in your search results and give customers more targeted results for multi-word search queries. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Common SEO Pitfalls in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: In addition to actively optimizing your site for search engines, you also want to avoid issues that commonly arise while developing and managing a website. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Compound Words
entries.headings: Simple Compound Words • Using Wildcards • Using Phrases • Using Synonyms with Compound Words
entries.summary: A compound word is a query term that a customer can enter as a single term or a phrase. If a compound word appears in a search phrase as a single term, it's automatically split. B2C Commerce treats each part of the split word as a separate search term. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Configure Custom Page Attributes for Page Designer
entries.summary: If the developer created custom page-level attributes for a page type, you can configure the attributes under Page Settings. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Change the ID for a Page Designer Page
entries.summary: Edit the ID for a Page Designer page. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Configure SEO Settings for a Page Designer Page
entries.summary: Enter SEO settings for a Page Designer page. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Find a Product Using Advanced Search
entries.summary: Find products in your catalog that have specific standard and custom attributes. Advanced search provides flexible, case-insensitive query options. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Configure the Auth Provider in the Sandbox Org
entries.summary: Before you begin, have the consumer key and secret generated by the external client available.

entries.title: Configure a Login Flow at the Primary Salesforce Org
entries.summary: To synchronize a user's identity between B2C Commerce and the Salesforce org IdP, configure a login flow at the primary Salesforce org. When a user signs in to the Salesforce IdP org, this flow handles the synchronization.

entries.title: Configure the User Interface for a Payment Processor
entries.summary: Use Business Manager to create a payment processor so customers can buy products using that processor. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Configure the Salesforce Sign-On URL in Business Manager
entries.summary: After you create an Auth Provider, Salesforce generates a single sign-on URL. Add this URL to the identity synchronization setting in Business Manager. These instructions apply whether Account Manager or the Salesforce org is the IdP.

entries.title: Configure Searchable Attributes
entries.summary: When a search query is entered in B2C Commerce, only product or content attributes that are configured as searchable attributes are searched, and only products or content assets that contain the search term in a searchable attribute are returned in search results. If the searched term is contained in an attribute that isn't a searchable attribute, the product isn't returned in the search results.

entries.title: Configure Single or Multiple-Code Coupons for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Create a single-code coupon or multiple-code coupons, depending on your B2C storefront's promotional needs. Single-code coupons use one code for all customers, multiple-code coupons use multiple unique codes. The code is what you share with customers through marketing campaigns.

entries.title: Configure System-Generated Coupon Codes for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: To auto-generate codes, first set the coupon code preferences, then configure the code settings. After you generate the codes, specify the coupons redemption conditions.

entries.title: Configure Variation Groups Display Mode
entries.summary: Enable the display settings for variation groups. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Configure 301 HTTP Redirects for Legacy URLs in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: 301 Redirects are used for permanent URL redirection, sending users to a different URL than the one they originally typed into their browser or selected from search engine results. The 301 redirect is considered a best practice for upgrading users from HTTP to HTTPS.

entries.title: Catalog-Level Search Refinement Definitions
entries.headings: Category Refinement Sorting Modes Example • Price Refinements • Promotion Refinements • Search Refinement Value Set • Search Refinement Buckets • Configure Catalog-Level Search Refinement Definitions • Localize Catalog-Level Search Refinement Definitions

keywords: To configure catalog-level search refinement definitions:

entries.title: Configure Change History Preferences for B2C Commerce

entries.title: Configure a Hostname Alias
entries.summary: A hostname alias is required to create short, meaningful URLs for external search engines to index. Assign at least one hostname alias that is the current hostname on which the instance is running.

entries.title: Configure Promotion Preferences for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Configure many of the promotion settings as site preferences, giving you much flexibility in how your promotions are handled. These settings include Buy X/Get Y promotion behavior, discount precision, and how the tax basis is calculated for line items with discounts.

entries.title: Configure Search Index Language Options
entries.summary: B2C Commerce indexes all allowed site locales by default. To reduce indexing times and overall resource consumption, exclude locales from indexing that don't have a particular set of data. You can also upload an extra dictionary for more directed and appropriate search results.

entries.title: Configure SEO URLs for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Optimizing site URLs for search engines improves user experience, rankings, linking functionality, and greatly increases site traffic.

entries.title: Configure Variation Attributes for Image Assignment by Catalog
entries.summary: B2C Commerce's catalog image settings support multiple variation attributes. The settings enable you to specify images for combinations of variation attributes, for example color (red, green, blue) and fabric (leather, textile) for all new products.

entries.title: Set Up B2C Commerce with a Salesforce Connection
entries.summary: Set up a trusted data exchange between B2C Commerce and a dedicated Salesforce org for the Merchant Agent to work. Connect your B2C Commerce instance to Salesforce to access agentic and cross-cloud experiences.

entries.title: Connect to Tableau
entries.summary: Connecting your B2C Commerce data to Tableau requires an API client ID. Use the Web Data Connector option in Tableau, and use the client ID to launch the connector and export your data to Tableau.

entries.title: Enable Salesforce Personalization for Shopper Notifications

Use Business Manager to create and schedule a slot configuration, instead of changing storefront code. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Controlling Promotions in B2C Commerce

entries.title: Create Product Bundles

entries.summary: Product options enable you to associate some products with other products so that customers can configure a customized product. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.summary: Configure a Page Designer page to be visible only to certain customers or only during certain dates and times. entries.title: Set Component Visibility by Customer Group, Schedule, or Locale
entries.summary: You can use Page Designer to control which components appear on a B2C Commerce storefront. You can select which components are visible to specific customer groups. You can also set components to appear only during certain times or for specific locales.

entries.title: Create 404 Error Pages with Alternate Paths in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Create error pages that let a customer get to the home page of your site, even if they experience a 404 error because they’re linking to an expired page or have a broken URL.

entries.title: Create a Campaign in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: A campaign acts as a wrapper for your promotions and qualifiers. When you create a campaign, define a schedule and qualifiers for an entire group of promotions or slot configurations.

entries.title: Create a Content Asset in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Create content assets that include text, images, and links via URLs. You can also import content assets from external sources. See Content Asset Types.

entries.title: Create a Customer Group in B2C Commerce

entries.title: Create a Dynamic Customer Group in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: A dynamic customer group is a group with changing membership. Membership is based on purchase history, profile, address, or active data. entries.title: Create a Library Folder in B2C Commerce

entries.title: Create a Search Index Rebuild Schedule
entries.summary: Use an index schedule to rebuild an index automatically and regularly in B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Create an A/B Test for B2C Commerce

entries.title: Create Campaigns and Promotions in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Creating a complete marketing and promotional campaign can seem like a daunting task, but doesn't have to be. Start by setting an overall goal and then break down the tasks involved.

entries.title: Create Canonical URL Tags for B2C Commerce

entries.title: Create Catalog and Content URLs
entries.summary: Configure how your SEO URLs are created for category URLs, category search refinement URLs, and product URLs.

entries.title: Create Content Search Refinements in B2C Commerce

entries.title: Create Customer Lists in B2C Commerce

If the database is less precise in location than you expect, it’s possible to create customer group rules that don't trigger correctly. entries.title: Create Feed Files

entries.title: Feeds for Sitemap Attributes in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Populate sitemap attribute values through the catalog and content import feeds, which let you set values for sitemap attributes of products and content respectively. entries.title: Create a Gift Certificate
entries.summary: Create gift certificates individually using Business Manager. Because you can't stage gift certificates, create and manage them in your Production environment. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Create Home Page Links

In this case, you can use a hostname-only URL instead of generating links to the homepage via a B2C Commerce pipeline. If you have a multi-locale site, ignore the rest of this topic.

entries.title: Create New Search Refinements

keywords: It's possible to include refinements for broad categories that are blocked for subcategories. If a customer navigates to the subcategory without selecting the blocked refinement, the blocked refinement isn't visible in the subcategory.

entries.title: Create an Order Promotion Rule in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: An order promotion rule applies to the entire order, for example, 20% off on all orders in April.

entries.title: Create Page Meta Tag Rules for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Using static and dynamic elements, define meta tags with a rule-based approach to create unique meta tag content and increase the SEO value across pages, products, and URLs.

entries.title: Page Designer for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Page Designer is a B2C Commerce feature that supports standard development processes and tools for creating reusable page types and component types. entries.title: Create Pipeline URLs

For SEO purposes, it's important to adjust the URL settings to create search-friendly URLs across your site. The standard B2C Commerce URL is not optimized for external search engines. entries.title: Create a Shipping Promotion Rule in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: A shipping promotion applies to shipping costs, for example Free shipping on orders over $100. Because a cart can contain multiple shipments, a product shipping promotion is applied to each shipment individually.

keywords: Sorting rules can also be assigned to keyword groups, which allow you to define extra sorting rules based on customer search terms.
Preview how sorting rules work in the storefront before using them.

entries.title: Create Stores in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use Business Manager to manage store location information, such as addresses, locations, and hours of business, which appears in your storefront.

entries.summary: Tiered discounts let shoppers qualify for higher value discounts for each new threshold they reach.

entries.summary: Further customize search-friendly URLs using custom pipelines.

entries.summary: Delete customers from a customer list as necessary.

entries.summary: Use Business Manager to delete up to 1000 customers at once using the standard method.

entries.title: Specify General Settings for B2C Commerce Coupons

entries.title: Edit or Delete Coupon Codes for B2C Commerce

entries.summary: Use Business Manager to edit the product details for an individual product. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Edit a Catalog

entries.summary: Use the B2C Commerce Reports & Dashboards Einstein Dashboard to review the performance of Einstein recommendations. The data can help you develop recommendation strategies that increase conversion and revenue.

entries.title: Enable Gift Certificate Functionality in Your Storefront
entries.summary: The SiteGenesis application comes with embedded gift certificate functionality, but you can also implement it on an existing application. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Enable Search-Friendly URLs
entries.summary: For your sitemap to include search-friendly URLs, turn them on them before creating your sitemap. Enable search-friendly URLs for your Production instance but not your Staging instance, because it makes it difficult to add content asset links based on URLs you want to link to.

entries.title: SEO Evaluation for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: There are various easy-to-use, low-cost (or free) tools on the Internet that can help provide you with a sense of how well your site is prepared for SEO. However, quantity doesn’t equal quality: in the end SEO is about driving qualified traffic that results in conversions.

entries.title: Exclude a Product from a Categorization Rule Set

entries.title: Export Your Catalog as a CSV File

entries.title: Export Customer Data Snapshots in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Export customer data snapshots to delete customer data from the system but retain an archive of it. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Extend an A/B Test in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Extend an A/B test to allow more time to collect results beyond the allotted time period.

entries.title: Find Orders
entries.summary: After an order is created in the storefront, view order details in Business Manager. Search using simple or advanced search parameters, or search by multiple order numbers. B2C Commerce performs full text case-insensitive search.

entries.title: Fully Additive and Semi-Additive Meausres

entries.title: Future Prices Preview in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use the Toolkit Preview to confirm future dated prices for products on your site. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Generate an API Client ID for Tableau Connector

entries.title: Generate an API Client ID for Marketing Cloud Intelligence

entries.title: Products Gift Certificate Dashboard
entries.summary: This topic applies to B2C Commerce. Use the Gift Certificate dashboard to analyze how your site generates revenue through gift certificate sales.

entries.summary: A hostname alias defines alternative names for an existing website URL. You must set up and register the DNS and domain names to use hostnames aliases in a B2C store. A hostname alias can map a human-readable hostname onto a more technical hostname. A hostname alias can also redirect one external, human-readable domain to another, such as www.mystore.com to www.yourstore.com.

entries.title: How B2C Commerce Assigns Page URLs
entries.headings: Product URLs • Content URLs
entries.summary: B2C Commerce provides several layers of inheritance URL attribute values that can be set through page-specific values, category values, or default values. URLs are assigned if the URL Rules module is enabled.

entries.title: How B2C Commerce Assigns SEO Support Page URLs
entries.headings: Product URLs • Content URLs
entries.summary: B2C Commerce provides several layers of inheritance URL attribute values that can be set through page-specific values, category values, or default values.

entries.title: Explicit Search Rank and Search Placement
entries.headings: How Search Rank and Search Placement inherit • Use Explicit Search Rank • Use Explicit Search Placement
entries.summary: B2C Commerce Explicit Search Rank and Placement allow you to promote a group of products and indicate the relevance of a particular product within a category.
keywords: If search rank values exist in your external catalog data, they can be set through the catalog import feed. Even if you intend to manually assign values, it's recommended that you assign a default value to all new products in your catalog import feeds. If you don't set a value, the value is set to zero and appears after any products explicitly ranked one (Low).

If search placement values exist in your external catalog data, they can be set through the catalog import feed. Even if you intend to manually assign values, it's recommended that you assign a default value to all new products in your catalog import feeds. If you don't set a value, the value is set to zero and appears after any one (NLA Products).

entries.title: How Sorting Rules Work
entries.headings: Order That Rules Apply and Breaking Ties • Direction • Inheritance • Variation Product Sorting Rules • Sorting Product Sets • Multi-Site Sorting Rules • Category Sorting Rules
entries.summary: B2C Commerce Sorting rules enable you to control the presentation of search results to the customer. Use sorting rules to bring products to the attention of customers by having them appear at the top of search results. Use any attribute to sort search results or a weighted blend of multiple attributes. To blend multiple attributes, create dynamic attributes.

entries.title: Hypernyms and Hyponyms
entries.headings: Negative Hypernyms
entries.summary: A hypernym is a broad term for a group of products. A hyponym is a term for an item in the group that the hypernym describes. A hypernym returns the results of all of its hyponyms, but the hyponyms only return their own results. Exclude specific hypernyms using negative hypernyms. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Image Management
entries.summary: Use B2C Commerce's Image Management System to create a matrix of images for each product.

entries.title: Optional community DIS product image wrapper
entries.summary: The optional open-source dis-product-image-wrapper project helps some Salesforce Reference Architecture (SFRA) and SiteGenesis projects centralize product image parameters and use JavaScript helpers. It isn't a required replacement for URLUtils and MediaFile from the B2C Commerce API.

entries.title: Responsive Images and Modern Image Formats (AVIF and WebP)
entries.summary: Load AVIF and WebP for smaller file sizes with modern browsers, and use JPEG (or PNG for lossless or transparency) as a fallback. Output formats, quality, and the URLUtils and MediaFile rules for DIS are in the Dynamic Imaging Service topic.

entries.title: Dynamic Imaging Service
entries.summary: The B2C Commerce Dynamic Imaging Service eliminates the need to upload different-sized images to meet your storefront requirements. After you upload a high-resolution source image, Dynamic Imaging formats the correct size for each image application—your product page, catalog page, recommended products, search results, and so on. Apply parameters to control image size, crop, overlay, format, background color, and quality settings.

entries.title: Troubleshooting and migration
entries.summary: When a B2C Commerce Dynamic Imaging Service image URL fails, times out, or looks wrong, check the source file, the full URL, and the limits documented in the Dynamic Imaging Service topic. This topic also helps you migrate from other hostnames and from Adobe Scene7 (Dynamic Media) query strings.

entries.title: Image Transformation URL Structure, Validation, and Order of Operations
entries.summary: Image transformation requests use a /dw/image/v2/ path and query string. The B2C Commerce Dynamic Imaging Service applies those parameters in a fixed order.

entries.title: Implementing a Choice of Bonus Product Discount in B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Rule-Based Discount • List-Based Discount • Example of Choice of Bonus Product Discount (List) with Prices • List-Based Discount Application
entries.summary: There are two types of choice of bonus products in the Salesforce B2C Commerce application: rule-based and list-based. Both are configurable and require application customization. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Import Boost and Bury Rules
entries.summary: Use search import and export to create, update, or replace boost and bury rules in bulk.

entries.title: Import Active Data .csv Files
entries.summary: Import Active Data .csv files to use production active data on your Sandbox instance or to test active data sorting rules. Download the daily active data feed from your Production instance and import it into your Sandbox.

entries.title: Import and Export Geolocations in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: B2C Commerce imports store geolocation data when you import a .zip file from external sources.

entries.title: Import and Export Price Books in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Import a price book as either an editable or a read-only price book. A read-only price book takes less time to import but you can't edit it in Business Manager. The default is editable.

entries.title: Import Feeds
entries.summary: To import active data into other instances, upload it in the production instance and then import it into another instance.

entries.title: Incremental Indexing
entries.headings: Data Updated by Incremental Indexing • Data Not Updated by Incremental Indexing • Tips and Tricks • Set Up Incremental Indexing
entries.summary: B2C Commerce indexes can be configured to rebuild incrementally, when product or category assignments are updated or on a schedule. If you enable incremental indexing for an index, then the index is automatically updated whenever a change is made to the products, content, or search configuration underlying the index. Changes to the index are available 30 seconds after a merchant makes a change. Incremental indexing can be enabled or disabled for each index type on each instance, but applies to all locales on the instance.
keywords: Set up incremental indexing for B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Index Creation
entries.headings: Product Index • Spelling Index • Content Index • Synonym Index • Suggest Index • Availability Index • Active Data Index
entries.summary: B2C Commerce search indexes use data from these indexes: Product, Spelling, Content, Synonym, Suggest, Availability, and Active data.

entries.title: Inventory Dashboard
entries.headings: Dashboard Metrics • Dashboard Reports
entries.summary: Use the B2C Commerce Inventory Dashboard to tracks inventory movement, in the Salesforce Omnichannel Inventory (OCI) service, by stock keeping unit (SKU). The metrics provide a view of your inventory that you can use to maintain inventory that meets customer demand.

entries.title: Keyword Searches for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Keyword searching is how most customers use your storefront to find the products they’re interested in buying. To create a large and accurate search index, specify the searchable product attributes and configure relationships using search dictionaries. When an item is searched for, B2C Commerce runs through a search process, gathers the results, and applies a sorting rule that defines how to display the respective results.

entries.title: Launch the Tableau Connector
entries.summary: From the Tableau desktop application, launch the connector as a web data connector to connect B2C Commerce data to Tableau.

entries.title: Line Item Tax Basis
entries.summary: Configure an alternative tax calculation for line items in an order with the B2C Commerce APIs (LineItem.updateTax ()) and Business Manager. This feature can be useful if your back-end systems can only handle product-level discounts but a line item requires you to prorate tax across an order.

entries.title: Link Categories for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Link categories to each other. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Link Products
entries.summary: Use the Links tab in the Product Editor to create links between products. Link from or to products. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Export Localizable Elements for Bulk Localization for Page Designer
entries.summary: To speed up the localization process, configure localization export and import using two API jobs. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Export Elements for Bulk Localization for Page Designer
entries.summary: To speed up the localization process, export the localizable elements of one or more pages with custom jobs. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Import Elements for Bulk Localization for Page Designer
entries.summary: To speed up the localization process, import the localized elements of one or more pages with custom jobs. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Export Localizable Elements Using Folders for Page Designer
entries.summary: To speed up your localization process, define pages in need of bulk translation. There are multiple ways to do this, such as by manually creating a file containing the page IDs in JSON string format. The simplest method is to assign pages to a dedicated content folder through Business Manager. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Manage Localization for a Page Designer Page
entries.summary: When adding a page in Page Designer, create it in your base language, typically your default locale. When you create the page, Page Designer adds a variation for each locale in the fallback hierarchy defined in Business Manager. Then use the page variations to localize components for each locale as needed. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Managing Product Options, Product Bundles, and Product Sets
entries.summary: You configure Product Options, Product Bundles, and Product Sets to groups products or products and variable options for sale on your storefront. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Base Product and Variation Group Scenarios
entries.headings: Scenario 1: Group and Sort Products in a Category by Color • Scenario 2: Assign Products to Slot Configuration Based on Color • Scenario 3: Assign Color Variation to Recommendation • Scenario 4: Show Swatches in One Category and Sliced Variations in Another Category • Scenario 5: Show Variation Groups, Variation Products, and Base Products with Swatches in a Category
entries.summary: The following scenarios describe different ways to present variation groups in your storefront. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.
keywords: Show products in a category based on color. For example, show swimsuit tops and bottoms together by color or accessories organized by color. In this scenario, you configure the T-shirt (TS) product to have a variation group based on size. Then you assign the variation groups, but not the base product, to the category.

In this scenario, you configure the T-shirt (TS) product to appear in three color variations. When you slice, the online variations products appear instead of the base product. You can show a different image for each variation of product, for example a coat in many colors. To show the different images, make the attribute a slicing attribute.

In This scenario, you configure the t-shirt product so that swatches for all available variations appear in a recommendation, whether they are assigned to the category or not. The scenario assumes the T-shirt (TS) product is set up for slicing. See Scenario 2 for more information. For more information about variation slicing by category.

In this scenario, you configure the T-shirt (TS) product to show a single image with swatches in the sale category and search results, but with sliced variations in the Women's Tops category. The search results determine slicing from the default on the Product page Variations tab. The sale category uses the default, while the Women's Top category overrides the default to allow slicing.

This scenario assigns blue and green variations to the Women's Top category and the amber variation to the sale category. If you worked through Scenario 4, skip assigning the product to the sale category.

entries.title: Base Product and Variation Product Scenarios
entries.headings: Scenario 1: Merchandise Using a Single Image with Swatches • Scenario 2: Merchandise Different Images for Each Variation (Slicing) • Scenario 3: Show Only Some Sliced Variations Per Category • Scenario 4: Show Swatches in One Category and Sliced Variations in Another Category • Scenario 5: Add a Variation Product to a Different Category
entries.summary: The following scenarios describe different ways to present variation products in your storefront. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.
keywords: In this scenario you merchandise a product with multiple colors or sizes using a single image, make sure that none of your variation attributes are set as a slicing attribute.
In this scenario, you configure the T-shirt (TS) product to appear in three color variations.
This scenario assumes the T-shirt (TS) product is set up for slicing. See Scenario 2 for more information.

In this scenario, you configure the T-shirt (TS) product to show a single image with swatches in the sale category and search results. In addition, sliced variations show in the Women's Tops category. The search results determine slicing from the default on the Product page Variations tab. The sale category uses the default, while the Women's Top category overrides the default to allow slicing.

This scenario assigns blue and green variations to the Women's Top category and the amber variation to the sale category. If you worked through Scenario 4, skip assigning the product to the sale category.

entries.summary: The Base Product and Variation Group Scenarios and Base Product and Variation Product Scenarios topics both use the products described here. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Manage Catalogs
entries.summary: Use B2C Commerce Business Manager to create, and edit catalogs, and assign catalogs to sites.

entries.title: Manage Component Visibility on Product List and Product Detail Template Page Designer Pages
entries.summary: On a Product List or Product Detail template page that uses category as its dynamic attribute, you can configure which components appear on a page based on the category being rendered. For example, specify that a certain component appears when the category is women’s clothes and a different component appears when the category is electronics. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Upload and Manage Files in the Content Library for Page Designer
entries.summary: Use the Media Manager to manage files in your content library. These files are available to you as you create Page Designer pages. (Available in the 24.4 release) you can add up to 1000 images per folder. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Manage Product Category Assignments
entries.summary: For the product to be visible in the storefront, you assign the product to a category. You assign the category to a catalog, and assign the catalog to a site.

entries.title: Manage Search Dictionaries
entries.summary: Create and manage all your personalized B2C Commerce Search Dictionary entries.

entries.title: Manage and Create Slot Configurations in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Salesforce B2C Commerce scans all templates to identify where slots have been defined. The resulting list appears on the Content Slots page.

entries.title: Manage Credit and Debit Cards in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use Business Manager to create and manage your credit and debit cards. The default credit and debit cards include: Visa, Master (Master Card), Discover, Diners Club (Diners Club International). This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Manage Images
entries.summary: Use the Image Management System to create a matrix of internal or external images that 's associated with products.

entries.title: Manage Orders in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: While you use a separate system to process orders, you can also use Business Manager for initial order creation and to manage order-related processes in B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Manage Payment Methods in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use Business Manager to create and manage your payment methods, which rely on the payment processors you have already created. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Manage Payment Processors in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use Business Manager to configure and manage a custom payment processor or use one or more pre-defined processors such as basic credit card, basic gift certificate, Cybersource BML (Bill Me Later), Cybersource Credit, PayPal Credit, PayPal Express, and Verisign credit. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Search Redirects
entries.headings: Managing Search Redirects • Search Redirect Options • Exact Match • Phrase Match • Broad Match • Negative Match • How Search Redirects Work • Localization • Create Search Redirects
entries.summary: Use the B2C Commerce guided search redirect feature to direct a customer to a particular page, URL, or pipeline when they enter a search term. When the customer searches on a term, for example, the redirect returns a specific page, instead of returning product search results for the entered search keyword. With search redirects, you have more control over what customers see, enhancing their buying experience, and directing them to content or products that increase the likelihood of a purchase.
keywords: You create search redirects to return a specific page, category, or URL when a customer searches on a particular keyword.

entries.title: Manage Shipping Methods in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use Business Manager to create and manage the shipping methods available in your site, including tax classes, currency, order value, and shipping cost. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Manage Site Taxes in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: The site taxation policy determines whether the site treats product and other prices as gross or net prices. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Managing Stale Data
entries.summary: Stale data is data that is older than a *fresh* period that you define. You define a fresh period for each feed. Each time a feed is imported, it resets the fresh period for any products in the feed. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Managing Stores in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Salesforce B2C Commerce supports the *Brick to Click* business model. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Manage Taxes in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: The site taxation policy determines if product and other prices are treated as gross or as net prices in the site. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Managing Variation Groups
entries.headings: Unassign Variation Groups • Variation Groups and Categories • Variation Groups and Property Inheritance
entries.summary: A variation group is a product type that represents a subset of variation products that are part of a base product. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Manually Migrate Active Data to a New Site
entries.summary: When your old source site and new target site are in the same realm, you can manually migrate your active data.

entries.title: Shopper Notification Channels and Templates
entries.summary: Access and customize notification templates and flows from the Notifications page in Business Manager.

entries.title: Create a Shopper Consent
entries.summary: Directly connect with your shoppers by getting permission to send marketing communications according to the terms that you outline in the consent request.

entries.title: Customize a Shopper Notification Channel Flow and Template
entries.summary: You can update a template with your site logo and colors to provide a branded experience. Notifications uses Salesforce Flow to trigger the shopper notification. The flows required for shopper notifications are created automatically when you install the flows and templates for the notification. To start sending notifications, activate the applicable flow in your Salesforce org.

entries.title: Define the Channels to Capture Shopper Consent
entries.summary: In Salesforce, select the channel types that you want to use to send notifications to shoppers, such as email, SMS, or WhatsApp. To use an engagement channel, create an attribute definition in your B2C Commerce instance and map it to the channel’s Salesforce record ID.

entries.title: Enable or Disable a Consent
entries.summary: After you enable a consent, it appears on your site in the consent components that you’ve configured.

entries.title: Install a Shopper Notification Channel Flow and Template
entries.summary: Channel settings vary by notification type. For example, the channel settings for Abandoned Cart Notification are different from the settings for Post Order Notification. Specify the channels to use.

entries.title: B2C Commerce with Agentforce Marketing
entries.summary: Connect B2C Commerce to your Salesforce org to use shopper consents and notifications powered by Agentforce Marketing, Data 360, and Personalization. Add consent requests to your B2C Commerce storefront to collect email addresses and build your Agentforce Marketing audience. Automate abandoned cart notifications via email, SMS, or WhatsApp. You can also create post-order marketing notifications with personalized recommendations that trigger when a shopper completes a purchase.

entries.title: Set Up Shopper Consents for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Add consent requests to your site to capture shopper emails and grow your marketing campaigns. Consent requests consume Data 360 credits. Contact your account executive to learn more about credit usage.

entries.title: Set Up an Email Domain for Shopper Notifications
entries.summary: Using Notifications requires a valid email address to indicate who the notification is from. You can use the default address provided by Salesforce or create an address using an authenticated site domain to provide a branded experience.

entries.title: Set Up Shopper Notifications for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Send shoppers notifications, such as reminders for abandoned carts or post-order marketing notifications. Notifications require Salesforce Flow, Agentforce Marketing, and Data 360. Data 360 credits are consumed for each notification sent. For information about credit usage, contact your account executive. You can also enable Personalization to customize your marketing communications to specific shopper needs and preferences.

entries.title: Prerequisites for B2C Commerce with Agentforce Marketing
entries.headings: Connect B2C Commerce to Salesforce • Map B2C Commerce Sites to Data 360 Data Spaces
entries.summary: Before you can use B2C Commerce with Agentforce Marketing, configure the required Salesforce apps and features.
keywords: Use the Salesforce Connection page to enable data exchange between your B2C Commerce instance and a dedicated Salesforce org.

A data space partitions your data for profile unification, insights, and marketing in Data 360. In Data 360, create a separate data space for each B2C Commerce site. Then in B2C Commerce, map each site to its corresponding Data 360 data space.

entries.title: Set Up a SLAS Client for Shopper Consents on PWA Storefront
entries.summary: If you have a PWA storefront, set up a Shopper Login and API Access Service (SLAS) client for your B2C Commerce instance to request access tokens.

entries.title: Display a Shopper Consent on a PWA or SFRA Storefront
entries.summary: You can display shopper consents in the marketing consent footer component of a Progressive Web App (PWA) site or the footer and registration components of a Storefront Reference Architecture (SFRA) site.

entries.title: Mask a Gift Certificate Code
entries.summary: A gift certificate code is sensitive data, so make sure that only the recipient has access to the code throughout the creation and redemption process. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Maximum Application for B2C Commerce Promotions
entries.headings: Maximum Application Limits for Choice of Bonus Product Discounts • Example
entries.summary: Limit how many times a promotion can apply per order using the maximum application limit.

entries.title: Merchandise by Product Variations
entries.summary: Variation Groups represent subsets of base products based on attributes like color or size, for example, all blue size-small items. Each variant must be its own product. Groups derive inventory from their SKUs, and function like sets or bundles in categories and promotions.

entries.title: Merchandising with the B2C Merchant Agent
entries.summary: After you set up the B2C Merchant Agent, use it to automate merchandising tasks. Instead of manually configuring ranking, sorting, and product categorization, use natural language prompts with the Merchant Agent to automate these tasks.

entries.title: Merchandising Your Site in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: As a merchant responsible for ecommerce at your organization, you have a depth of experience in your industry and brand that you can apply to your storefront in B2C Commerce. Use Salesforce B2C Commerce to control most of the configuration of your site directly through Business Manager.

entries.title: Merchant Agent for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: The Merchant Agent for B2C Commerce is an AI-powered assistant in Business Manager that automates merchandising tasks such as sorting rules, boost-and-bury configurations, and categorization for merchant admins. Merchant admins describe their intent in natural language, and the agent generates configurations that they can review and apply.

entries.title: Merchant Agent License Requirements
entries.summary: Review the licenses required to use the Merchant Agent with B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Merchant Agent Setup for B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Merchant Agent Setup Checklist
entries.summary: To use the Merchant Agent with B2C Commerce and the connected Salesforce org, complete the required setup tasks. The instructions assume that you are a B2C Commerce admin and Salesforce admin responsible for merchandising in your organization.

entries.title: Meta Tag Syntax for B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Static Text • Dynamic Text • Convenience Objects • OR Expression • AND Expression • ELSE Expression • IF-THEN Expression • EQ Expression
entries.summary: The rule syntax supports the following different operations that are optimized for page meta tag content generation. Also, script operators are not valid for each scope. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Meta Tag Rules for B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Rule Inheritance • Script API • Import and Export • Best Practices
entries.summary: Page meta tags are used in HTML documents to provide structured data about a web page. Depending on your requirements and integrations, manage your content to provide meaningful data for those tags. B2C Commerce provides a rule-based solution that enables you to generate and manage the corresponding content for your storefront.

entries.title: Site Integration Meta Tag Rules for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use these APIs to obtain page meta tags that generate based on rules. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Modify Product Display Order in Grid View
entries.summary: Arrange products on your product page and see the changes in your storefront. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Modify Category Display Order for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Modify the display order of the categories within the catalog to define the structure for products and navigation in the storefront. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Modify Product Display Order for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Modify the display order of the products within a category in the storefront. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Monitor Product Readiness
entries.summary: View insights into the readiness status of the products in your catalog from the Merchant Tools Home page or the product list.

entries.title: Multi-Currency Sites in B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Session Currency • Reports and Analytics • Implementation Considerations • Import and Export for Multiple-Currency Sites • APIs for Multi-Currency Sites • Feature Limitation to Be Addressed in Future Releases
entries.summary: The Salesforce B2C Commerce multi-currency sites feature enables you to sell products in multiple countries or regions. You use the feature so that your customers can browse products and checkout carts in their preferred purchase currencies.

entries.title: Multi-Select Attribute Search Refinements
entries.summary: In B2C Commerce, set up search refinements that enable your customers to select multiple attribute refinement options at once, instead of one value at a time. Customers can select multiple brands that they want to consider. Selecting one brand option immediately limits the search result to that brand, but keeps the other brand options in the user interface to allow broadening of the search result.

entries.title: Redemption Limits for Multiple Coupons in the Same B2C Commerce Order
entries.summary: Shoppers can apply multiple coupon codes in one B2C Commerce order.

entries.title: Notify Search Engines About Your B2C Commerce Sitemap
entries.summary: After successfully creating a sitemap for a storefront, submit the sitemap to main search engines manually. Register with the Google Sitemaps program and submit your sitemap. Use this program also to see some interesting statistics about your site, such as the most used keywords and any spidering problems.

entries.title: Null Values in Sorting Rules
entries.headings: Standard Attributes • Dynamic Attributes
entries.summary: For standard attributes, null values are handled identically for all data types in B2C Commerce. For dynamic attributes, configure whether you want a null value to be interpreted as a minimum, maximum, or average value using the dynamic attribute Default Value option.

entries.title: On-Site Search Top Search Dashboard
entries.summary: The B2C Commerce Reports & Dashboards On-Site Search–Top Search dashboard reflects how customers use search on your storefront. The metrics provide an accurate view of the frequency of search and the use of search terms.

entries.title: Read-Only Price Books in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Read-only price books import faster than standard price books at the cost of editability. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Order Origination Attributes
entries.summary: This topic applies to B2C Commerce. As part of your omni-channel strategy, you can get a detailed breakdown of how your orders originate in GMV reports.

entries.title: Order Promotions in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Use Salesforce B2C Commerce to configure order promotions for percentage discounts, fixed price discounts, and free shipping. Offer a bonus product or a choice of bonus products, and tier order discounts. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Orders in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: You can use Business Manager to manage many aspects of your B2C Commerce site's order processing requirements. For example, you can modify order and payment information, edit order attributes, add a note to an order record, send emails to confirm an order status, cancel an order, or export orders.

entries.title: Override Generated SEO URLs
entries.summary: If the URL Rules module enabled, you can use the Page URL attribute to override the URL that is generated from the configuration in the URL Rules module. You can apply character replacement for URLs created with this override.

entries.title: Copy Page Designer Components
entries.summary: Copy up to 10 components at a time. Paste a copied component into any region or component that accepts it. Copying components helps make sure consistent site-wide experiences when building Page Designer pages.

entries.title: Organize Page Designer Pages in Folders
entries.summary: Assign Page Designer pages to one or more content library folders. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Page Meta Tag Rules for B2C Commerce
entries.summary: Page meta tags are used in HTML documents to provide structured data about a web page. Common tags are title, robots, description, and social tags such as open graph tags. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Page Meta Tags for B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Meta Title and Page Title • Meta Description • Robots Meta Tag • Additional Page Meta Tag Resources
entries.summary: Page meta tags are used in HTML documents to provide structured data about a web page. Meta tags are snippets of text that don't appear on the page itself, but in the page’s code, describing a page’s content. Common tags are title, robots, description, and social tags such as open graph tags. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Bind Component Attributes to Content Assets
entries.summary: Bind a component attribute to a content asset attribute, allowing the component to dynamically display the content asset's data. If you later update the content asset, the changes appear automatically on every component that uses it.

entries.title: Content Asset Data Binding in Page Designer Overview
entries.headings: How Data Binding Works • Benefits of Data Binding • Binding to a Component • Which Content Asset Attributes Can You Bind? • Other Considerations
entries.summary: Content asset data binding allows Page Designer components to dynamically pull content from content assets instead of using static, manually entered values. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Binding Components to Content Assets
entries.summary: Instead of entering content directly into a component, bind the component's attributes to content asset attributes. Use this approach to reuse content across multiple pages and update it from a single location. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Page Designer Visual Editor Tips
entries.summary: The following tips have been collected through customer feedback, and are helpful to know when building Page Designer pages using the Visual Editor. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

Your base product catalog can also be your storefront catalog or you can create a separate storefront catalog. You organize a catalog by categories. Categories can act as the storefront navigation to your products. For a product or service to show on your storefront, assign it to a category. Create any number of catalog, category, and product scenarios to address your business needs. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.summary: Promotion priority rules exist to prevent unpredictable or undesirable effects when multiple promotions are applicable for a basket. These rules are hardcoded but you can adjust certain promotion attributes, such as exclusivity and rank, to manipulate promotions application. B2C Commerce doesn't evaluate all sequences of promotions. The sequencing resulting from prioritization doesn't always provide the best value to shoppers. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Promotion Proration Logic

entries.summary: Availability Ranking describes an approach of merchandising using the functionality and power of an online product search, expanding the online merchandising activities of merchants to one of the most important tools in an online shop today, the product search. You can use availability ranking to manipulate the search results of a customer query to reflect what you want to highlight. For example, you might want to promote in-stock products, and push products with low stock levels to the end of the search results. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.
keywords: Merchants want to be able to rank certain products higher (or lower) in search results based on their own criteria.

entries.title: Search Refinement for B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Condensing Multiple Search Refinement Values • Refinement Inheritance • Deepest Common Category
entries.summary: With B2C Commerce, you can let customers refine a search using multiple attributes, such as price, promotion, category, or a product attribute. For example, let customers search for TVs based on-screen size, price and manufacturer, or to search for lenses based on zoom magnification, price, and manufacturer. This is useful if you have a large catalog and any particular customer is only looking for specific products. For example, on an apparel site, most customers are not interested in items that are not in their desired size or color.

entries.headings: AI-Powered Search and Discoverability • Site Design Checklist • Site Configuration Checklist • Deployment Checklist • Maintenance
entries.summary: Search Engine Optimization (SEO) focuses on increasing non-paid search engine results for your site. Utilizing both creative and technical elements, you can improve search engine ranking and awareness, with the goal of driving more traffic to your site. External search engines (for example, Google) assign lower ranking to lengthy URLs with non-relevant keywords, multiple directory layers, and parameters. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Sitemaps in B2C Commerce
entries.headings: Configuring Sitemaps in B2C Commerce • Home Page Link in Sitemap • Multiple Sites
entries.summary: Sitemaps are XML files that provide search engines, such as Google and Yahoo with information about a website so crawler can index it more efficiently. Sitemaps contain a list of all the URLs available for indexing and can provide the search engine with additional information, such as when a page was last updated, frequency of updates, and the relevance of a page. Search engines use this information to construct links to your site and control the ranking of links within its search results. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: Sitemap Topology in B2C Commerce
entries.summary: To include your site in its search results, Google uses a googlebot to crawl your site. Google knows what pages are new in your site using the sitemap you provide. When Google requests the sitemap, the SiteMap pipeline in your application serves the sitemap to Google, using the SendGoogleSiteMap pipelet. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.

entries.title: SKU Coverage
entries.headings: Standard Product • Base Product • Product Bundle • Product Set
entries.summary: SKU Coverage is the normalized ratio between the total number and the number of available variations of a base product. SKU Coverage is the same for all sites that share an Inventory list, so it's a site-specific value. This topic applies to B2C Commmerce.

entries.summary: Synonyms are words or phrases that mean exactly or nearly the same as another word or phrase. If a number of terms are defined as synonyms, then searching for one of those terms returns the results for all of the terms. You can also exclude specific synonyms using negative synonyms. Brand synonyms are automatically generated. This topic applies to B2C Commerce.
keywords: If you enter synonyms (single or multi-word) with the '-' negative synonym operator in front of it, products containing the negative synonym are *not* returned in the search results.

B2C Commerce automatically generates an internal dictionary of brand names from the values in the product system object brand attribute. Marking the brand attribute as searchable isn't required for the dictionary to be created. If B2C Commerce detects a misspelled brand name in the search query, it adds the correct brand name from the internal dictionary to the query as a synonym.

entries.title: Understanding VAT Implications
entries.headings: Storefront Customization
entries.summary: This topic applies to B2C Commerce. The value added tax (VAT) is a form of indirect sales tax paid on products and services at each stage of production or distribution, based on the value added at that stage and included in the cost to the ultimate customer. At each stage, the tax is assessed as a percentage on the amount by which goods increase in value from the raw materials or semi-manufactured stage.

If your site experiences these discrepancies, use unit-level rounding.

entries.title: Upgrade from SEO Support to URL Rules
entries.headings: Test URL Rules in a Sandbox • Configure URL Rules • Auto-Redirect of Updated URLs
entries.summary: If you implemented your site before Release 13.1, this topic contains the information you need to upgrade to the new module. In Release 13.1, B2C Commerce added the URL Rules module, which enables you to customize storefront URLs to optimize search engine results. The SEO Support feature, which provided a limited version of the same functionality, was deprecated for 13.1 and is planned for removal at a future date. The SEO Support functionality is accessible under the URL Rules module, until the new URL Rules module is enabled using the site preference for Storefront URLs.
keywords: To use the new URL Rules feature, turn it on. The URL Rules module shows the SEO Support page until the URL Rules module is enabled. This is a one-way transition and no redirects are created if you decide to disable URL Rules and reenable the deprecated SEO Support feature. Test your new configuration on Sandboxes before enabling the feature on Staging or Production.
When you are confident that you can transition to the new URL Rules feature, enable it on your Staging instance and import your configuration from your Sandbox instance.

Old URLs refer to URLs configured prior to Release 13.1, whether they are search-friendly URLs configured through the SEO Support module or standard B2C Commerce URLs not optimized for external search engines. New URLs refer to URLs configured using the URL Rules module. In Release 13.1, new URLs are disabled by default for existing sites and are enabled for sites created after the release. Old URLs work when the URL Rules module is disabled, and are redirected when the URL Rules module is enabled.
